ExpectedTimetable.name: Expected Timetable
ExpectedTimetable.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present the IPO timetable as a precision timetable and compliance control schedule. Use exact dates and local times; precision, not marketing tone.

Required structure:
- Lead-in note that the timetable may change and announcements will be published.
- Table or bullet timeline with event, date, time, and note fields.
- Include application opening and closing, pricing, results and allotment, share certificate validity or CCASS crediting, and expected trading commencement if supported.
- State that all dates and times are Hong Kong time unless otherwise stated.
- Include severe weather or extreme conditions arrangements if supported.

Controls:
- Treat this as a factual timing section, not marketing copy.
- Keep every event line even if incomplete; for missing dates or times use [Information not provided in the documents].
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] and cross-references to Structure of the Global Offering and application procedures (How to Apply) where timetable items depend on offering mechanics.
- If application procedures are not separately available, add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm application channel timing, severe weather arrangements, and results publication process.]].
ExpectedTimetable.kg_function: To outline the schedule of key events in the Hong Kong Public Offering.
ExpectedTimetable.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and clarity regarding the timeline of the offering process, allowing investors to plan accordingly. Regulators and investors expect to understand when critical steps occur, such as the start and end of the application period, price determination, and the release of allocation results.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Commencement of Hong Kong Public Offering
kg_typical_structure.description: The exact time and day when the public offering begins.
The last time electronic applications can be submitted.
The time when application lists are made available to the public.
The last time payments for applications and instructions to HKSCC must be completed.
The exact time when applications lists are closed.
The date when the offer price is determined.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Announcement of Offer Price and Other Details
kg_typical_structure.description: The date and method of announcing the offer price and other relevant details.
The date and method of releasing the results of allocations.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Dispatch/Collection of Share Certificates and Refunds
kg_typical_structure.description: The date and method of dispatching or collecting share certificates and refunds.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and precise language.
Ensure all times and dates are accurate and consistent.
Cross-reference with other sections such as 'Structure of the Global Offering' and 'How to Apply for Hong Kong Offer Shares'.
Include any changes to the timetable in subsequent announcements.
Adhere to the format and style guidelines provided by HKEX.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date when the Hong Kong Public Offering starts.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 9:00 a.m. on Tuesday, December 7, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The last time electronic applications can be submitted.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 11:30 a.m. on Friday, December 10, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The time when application lists are made available.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 11:45 a.m. on Friday, December 10, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The last time payments for applications and instructions to HKSCC must be completed.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 12:00 noon on Friday, December 10, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The exact time when applications lists are closed.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 12:00 noon on Friday, December 10, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date when the offer price is determined.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Friday, December 10, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date and method of announcing the offer price.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Wednesday, December 16, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date and method of releasing the results of allocations.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Wednesday, December 16, 2021

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date and method of dispatching or collecting share certificates and refunds.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Or before such other date as notified by us as the date of dispatch/collection of Share certificates/e-Refund payment instructions/refund checks.

kg_common_pitfalls: Inconsistent or incorrect dates and times.
Failing to update the timetable if changes occur.
Omitting important steps in the process.
Not providing sufficient detail for each step.
Failing to cross-reference with other sections.

Contents.name: Contents
Contents.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a hierarchical table of contents for the prospectus. Headings here must match the final document exactly.

Required structure:
- Exact heading list in final order: front matter, risk and compliance chapters, business and financial chapters, offering chapters, and appendices if supported.
- Use section titles exactly as they appear in the generated document. No missing headings; no orphan sections that are referenced elsewhere but absent here.
- Page mapping or section references; if pagination is unavailable, keep the entry and mark the page field as [Information not provided in the documents].

Controls:
- No orphan sections: if a section is listed here it must appear elsewhere, and vice versa. Every heading referenced in the body must appear in Contents.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=all section headings]] or equivalent notes wherever heading alignment must be checked.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Final pagination and heading consistency check required before filing.]].
Contents.kg_function: To outline the organization of the prospectus and guide readers to the relevant sections.
Contents.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and ease of navigation for investors. It serves as a roadmap, allowing readers to quickly find the information they need regarding the offering, including important disclaimers, key financial details, and other critical sections like future plans and use of proceeds.

kg_typical_structure.description: Contains disclaimers and legal notices about the prospectus and the securities being offered.
Provides a timeline for the offering process, including key dates and milestones.
Offers a brief overview of the company, its business, and the offering.
Lists and explains key terms and acronyms used throughout the prospectus.
Describes the company’s history, reorganizations, and current corporate structure.
Details the company’s business operations, strategies, and market position.
Includes audited and unaudited financial statements and other financial data.
Discusses relationships between the company and its controlling shareholders.
Details any significant transactions involving related parties.
Provides information on the company’s share capital structure.
Lists major shareholders and their stakes in the company.
Describes the board of directors and senior management team.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Future Plans and Use of Proceeds
kg_typical_structure.description: Outlines the company’s future plans and how the proceeds from the offering will be used.
Details the cornerstone investors and their commitments.
Explains the underwriting arrangements and the role of underwriters.
Describes the structure of the global offering, including the roles of various parties.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: How to Apply for Hong Kong Offer Shares
kg_typical_structure.description: Provides instructions on how to apply for the Hong Kong offer shares.
Includes additional documents such as reports, financial information, and legal summaries.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Ensure all disclaimers and legal notices are accurate and up-to-date.
Cross-reference important information within the prospectus.
Maintain a consistent format and style throughout the document.
Avoid using technical jargon unless necessary and define it clearly.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Key dates and milestones for the offering process.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Application period: 1st January 2023 - 31st January 2023; Listing date: 1st February 2023

kg_required_input_fields.description: Brief overview of the company, its business, and the offering.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Our company is a leading provider of AI solutions in the technology sector.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Audited and unaudited financial statements and other financial data.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Annual financial statements for the years ended 31 December 2021 and 2020.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Detailed plans for the use of proceeds from the offering.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Proceeds will be used to expand our R&D capabilities and market presence.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Details of cornerstone investors and their commitments.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Cornerstone investor A committed to invest $10 million in the offering.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Information on underwriting arrangements and the role of underwriters.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Underwriting agreement with XYZ Investment Bank.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Description of the structure of the global offering, including the roles of various parties.
kg_required_input_fields.example: The offering will be structured as a global offering with joint bookrunners and lead managers.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Instructions on how to apply for the Hong Kong offer shares.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Applications can be submitted online through the company’s website.

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to include all required disclaimers and legal notices.
Inconsistent formatting and style throughout the document.
Omitting important financial information or misrepresenting data.
Neglecting to update information that is outdated or incorrect.
Failing to properly cross-reference important information.

Summary.name: Summary
Summary.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a high-level, balanced executive summary of the issuer and the offering. Balanced factual tone only.

Required structure:
- Opening purpose statement explaining that the summary should be read with the full prospectus.
- Business and issuer overview (high-level only).
- Industry and market context only if supported.
- Key operating and financial information, preferably in concise tables if the data supports it.
- Material strengths stated neutrally and only if evidenced.
- Short risk summary with [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]]. No new risk themes that are absent from Risk Factors.
- Offering overview and recent developments if supported.

Controls:
- No marketing hype, no superlatives unless tightly scoped and supported.
- Every numeric claim must reconcile to Business or Financial Information or be replaced with [Information not provided in the documents].
- Every market claim (rank, market share, CAGR, leadership) must be tagged with [[AI:CITE|source=...; scope=...; date=...; metric=...]] or be omitted.
- Do not quantify future profitability, margins, or valuation outcomes.
Summary.kg_function: To provide a high-level overview of the key aspects of the issuer and the offering, ensuring investors understand the core business, risks, and use of proceeds.
Summary.kg_purpose: HKEX requires the Summary section to ensure that investors have a clear understanding of the key aspects of the issuer and the offering without delving into detailed technical or financial information. This helps investors make informed decisions and ensures transparency and fairness in the IPO process.

kg_typical_structure.description: Brief introduction to the issuer, highlighting its core business, market position, and key achievements.
Detailed description of the issuer’s business, including its products, services, market position, and competitive landscape.
Summary of the main risks associated with the investment, cross-referencing to the full risk factors section.
Description of how the proceeds from the offering will be used, aligned with the issuer’s strategic plans.
Summary of key financial metrics, such as revenue, profit margins, and growth trends.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Avoid technical jargon and overly complex sentences.
Provide factual information and avoid subjective opinions.
Cross-reference to other sections of the prospectus where appropriate.
Ensure consistency with the information provided in other sections of the prospectus.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Name of the company issuing the shares.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Amind Holdings Limited

kg_required_input_fields.description: Brief description of the issuer’s business, products, and services.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Leading AI software company with a focus on computer vision technologies.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Details on the issuer’s market position and competitive landscape.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Largest AI software company in Asia, with a market share of 11% in China.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Summary of the main risks associated with the investment.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Risks related to technological obsolescence and regulatory changes.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Description of how the proceeds from the offering will be used.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Enhancing R&D capabilities, commercialization capabilities, and potential strategic investments.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Key financial metrics, such as revenue, profit margins, and growth trends.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Revenue of HK$1,000 million in the last fiscal year, with a 10% year-over-year growth.

kg_common_pitfalls: Overgeneralizing or oversimplifying the business and risks.
Failing to cross-reference to other sections of the prospectus.
Including outdated or incorrect financial information.
Neglecting to highlight key risks that could impact the investment.
Providing too much detail that distracts from the overall summary.

Definitions.name: Definitions
Definitions.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide the formal Definitions section as a dictionary-style list. Exact wording consistency with document-wide usage.

Required structure:
- Dictionary-style output: alphabetical list of defined terms.
- Include company entities, key people where defined, regulators, legal and financial abbreviations, and recurring deal terms if supported.
- Maintain prospectus-style precise wording rather than narrative prose.

Controls:
- Do not invent definitions for terms not supported by the documents. Remove unnecessary defined terms; define only terms that are actually used or clearly need standardised meaning.
- Jurisdiction definitions must be used consistently across the document.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm each defined term matches actual usage throughout the draft.]] where helpful.
Definitions.kg_function: To define key terms and acronyms used throughout the prospectus to ensure clarity and consistency.
Definitions.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure that all terms and acronyms used in the prospectus are clearly defined to prevent ambiguity and ensure that investors and regulators understand the precise meanings of these terms. This section helps maintain transparency and facilitates a smooth reading experience for the audience.

kg_typical_structure.description: Lists of terms and their definitions, often organized alphabetically or by relevance.
References to other sections where related terms are defined or explained in more detail.
Notes on how rounding has affected certain figures and the implications thereof.
Notes on the use of Chinese and English names for entities, with a preference for Chinese versions in case of inconsistencies.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Define terms consistently throughout the document.
Cross-reference to glossary or other relevant sections.
Include rounding notes if applicable.
Use consistent formatting for all definitions.
Ensure all acronyms are expanded on first use.
Use the same term for the same concept throughout the document.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The term to be defined.
kg_required_input_fields.example: affiliate

kg_required_input_fields.description: The definition of the term.
kg_required_input_fields.example: any other person, directly or indirectly, controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such specified person

kg_required_input_fields.description: The acronym to be expanded.
kg_required_input_fields.example: CAC

kg_required_input_fields.description: The full name of the entity or organization.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Cyberspace Administration of China

kg_required_input_fields.description: Note on rounding of figures and its impact.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Figures shown as totals in certain tables may not be an arithmetic aggregation of the figures preceding them.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Section or part of the document where the term is further explained.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Appendix III to this Prospectus

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to define terms consistently throughout the document.
Overlooking rounding notes and their impact on figures.
Not providing cross-references to other relevant sections.
Using inconsistent formatting for definitions.
Failing to expand acronyms on first use.
Including outdated or incorrect definitions.

Glossary.name: Glossary of Technical Terms
Glossary.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Explain technical or industry-specific terms in plain language for investors. Consistency with Business and Regulatory Overview.

Required structure:
- Alphabetical list of technical terms or acronyms. Plain-language explanations only.
- Each entry should be concise, plain-language, and non-promotional.
- Include only technical concepts that are relevant to the business and used elsewhere in the draft.

Controls:
- Do not duplicate legal or entity definitions that belong in Definitions. Avoid circular or self-referential explanations.
- Risk-sensitive technical terms (e.g. data, privacy, cybersecurity, model training, internet information services) must carry [[AI:XREF|to=Business]], [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]], or [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where appropriate.
- For missing technical definitions keep the term and use [Information not provided in the documents].
Glossary.kg_function: To define and explain technical terms used throughout the prospectus to ensure clarity and consistency.
Glossary.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure that all technical terms used in the prospectus are clearly defined and consistent with the issuer's intended meaning. This helps investors and regulators understand the issuer's business and technology accurately, reducing ambiguity and potential misinterpretation.

kg_typical_structure.description: Each term is defined with a clear and concise explanation.
Where applicable, examples and context are provided to illustrate how the term is used in practice.
Cross-references to related terms or sections are included where relevant.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Define terms consistently with industry standards when possible.
Avoid overly technical jargon unless necessary.
Include cross-references to related terms or sections.
Ensure definitions align with the issuer's intended meaning.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The technical term to be defined.
kg_required_input_fields.example: AI

kg_required_input_fields.description: A clear and concise definition of the term.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Artificial intelligence, an area of computer science that focuses on machinery simulation of intelligence displayed by humans and other animals.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Any additional context or examples to clarify the term's usage.
kg_required_input_fields.example: AI is used in our company to develop autonomous intelligent entities leveraging LLM technology.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Any related terms or sections that should be cross-referenced.
kg_required_input_fields.example: See also 'AI Agent', 'AI Infrastructure'

kg_common_pitfalls: Overuse of technical jargon without clear definitions.
Inconsistent definitions across the prospectus.
Lack of cross-references to related terms or sections.
Failure to update definitions if the meaning changes post-filing.

ForwardLooking.name: Forward-Looking Statements
ForwardLooking.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide a formal cautionary disclaimer for forward-looking statements. Legal cautionary tone.

Required structure:
- Explain what forward-looking statements are; include examples of forward-looking language where appropriate.
- State that such statements are based on assumptions and currently available information.
- Warn that actual results may differ materially.
- Caution investors not to place undue reliance on such statements.
- State that the statements speak only as of the date of the prospectus and the issuer undertakes no obligation to update except as required by law.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]].

Controls:
- Use formal legal language and neutral examples only. Explicit warning: do not embed unqualified future language elsewhere in the document; this section is the designated place for forward-looking caveats.
- Do not include express or implied profit forecasts, margin forecasts, or dated profitability claims.
- If company-specific future plans are not evidenced, do not invent them; use generic safe-harbour wording and [Information not provided in the documents] where necessary.
ForwardLooking.kg_function: To disclose potential risks and uncertainties associated with forward-looking statements in the prospectus.
ForwardLooking.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and protect investors by clearly identifying statements that are speculative in nature. It helps prevent misinterpretation of the company's future expectations and aligns with regulatory standards for disclosure practices.

kg_typical_structure.description: Lists all forward-looking statements in the prospectus and identifies them using specific language.
Discusses various risks and uncertainties that could affect the accuracy of forward-looking statements.
Provides a disclaimer to caution readers against placing undue reliance on forward-looking statements.
States the company's obligation (or lack thereof) to update forward-looking statements.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Identify forward-looking statements using specific phrases like 'believe', 'expect', 'anticipate', etc.
List all forward-looking statements comprehensively.
Provide a detailed discussion of risks and uncertainties.
Include a disclaimer to caution against relying on forward-looking statements.
State the company's obligation (or lack thereof) to update forward-looking statements.

kg_required_input_fields.description: List of all forward-looking statements in the prospectus.
kg_required_input_fields.example: We believe our revenue will grow by 20% next year.

kg_required_input_fields.description: List of factors that could impact the accuracy of forward-looking statements.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Economic downturn, regulatory changes, competition, etc.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Text of the disclaimer to caution against relying on forward-looking statements.
kg_required_input_fields.example: We do not undertake any obligation to update or revise any forward-looking statements.

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to identify all forward-looking statements.
Overstating the certainty of forward-looking statements.
Neglecting to discuss significant risks and uncertainties.
Not providing a clear disclaimer.
Failing to update the section if there are changes in the company's situation.

RiskFactors.name: Risk Factors
RiskFactors.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Present material applicant-specific risks in a frank, structured, and disclosure-focused manner. One principal risk per heading; preserve individual risk specificity before any grouping.

Required structure:
- Use one principal risk per sub-heading. Under each sub-heading explain the factual cause, how the risk harms the issuer or investors, and quantification where possible. If quantification is not available from the documents, say so explicitly rather than guessing.
- Group by sensible risk type (business and industry, regulation and compliance, financial condition, technology or data, offering or shareholder risks) only after individual risk specificity is preserved.

Controls:
- No mitigation language inside the same risk narrative; place any mitigation in other sections and use [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] or [[AI:XREF|to=Regulation]] where appropriate.
- No vague unknown-risks boilerplate.
- For technology issuers, include supported risks relating to commercialisation, data privacy and cybersecurity, IP ownership, model performance or safety, third-party dependency, WVR, or 18C where applicable.
- Unsupported required risks should remain as headings with [Information not provided in the documents] plus [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain issuer-specific facts and quantification for this risk.]].
RiskFactors.kg_function: To disclose material risks that could impact the issuer's business, financial condition, and results of operations, thereby enabling investors to make informed decisions.
RiskFactors.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and full disclosure of potential risks that could affect the issuer's business. This helps protect investors by providing them with comprehensive information about the risks associated with investing in the company's securities, ensuring they can make informed investment decisions based on a thorough understanding of the risks involved.

kg_typical_structure.description: Briefly introduces the risk factors section and reminds readers to consider all information in the document, especially the risks mentioned.
Discusses risks related to the industry in which the issuer operates, such as technological obsolescence, competition, and regulatory changes.
Details risks related to the day-to-day operations of the issuer, such as supply chain disruptions, cybersecurity threats, and labor issues.
Outlines financial risks, including liquidity issues, credit risks, and the impact of economic conditions on the issuer's financial health.
Describes risks related to compliance with laws and regulations, including the possibility of fines, penalties, or operational disruptions due to non-compliance.
Addresses risks associated with market conditions, such as fluctuations in exchange rates, commodity prices, and market volatility.
Includes any other significant risks that are not covered in the above subsections but are material to the issuer's business.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Avoid vague or overly general statements.
Provide specific examples to illustrate risks.
Cross-reference relevant sections of the prospectus where appropriate.
Disclose all material risks, even if they are unlikely to occur.
Update the section with the latest information available.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Name of the issuing company.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Tech Innovations Ltd.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Date up to which the information in the risk factors section is current.
kg_required_input_fields.example: 31 December 2022

kg_required_input_fields.description: List of industry-specific risks faced by the issuer.
List of operational risks faced by the issuer.
List of financial risks faced by the issuer.
List of regulatory risks faced by the issuer.
List of market risks faced by the issuer.
Any other significant risks that are material to the issuer's business.

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to disclose all material risks, even if they are unlikely to occur.
Using overly general or vague language to describe risks.
Not updating the section with the latest information available.
Failing to cross-reference relevant sections of the prospectus.
Omitting important details that could affect the reader's understanding of the risks.

Waivers.name: Waivers from Strict Compliance with Listing Rules (Waivers and Exemptions)
Waivers.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Disclose any waivers or exemptions from strict compliance with Listing Rules or prospectus requirements.

Required structure (for each waiver): rule; normal requirement; issuer-specific reason for non-compliance; waiver granted; conditions; investor relevance; documentary verification hook (evidence reference for approval and conditions).
- Cite rule numbers and titles where supported.
- If no waiver is disclosed in the documents, say so expressly.

Controls:
- Do not say a waiver was granted unless the documents support that conclusion.
- If a waiver signals structural or compliance risk, add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]].
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain Exchange approval evidence and confirm compliance with any conditions.]] where details are incomplete.
- Keep unsupported line items as [Information not provided in the documents] rather than inferring the waiver package.
Waivers.kg_function: To detail any waivers or exemptions obtained from the strict compliance with the Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing Limited (HKEX) Listing Rules or other relevant ordinances.
Waivers.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and to address any deviations from the standard requirements, providing investors with a clear understanding of the rationale behind these deviations. This section helps maintain market integrity and ensures that the issuer meets the necessary regulatory standards where possible.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Waiver in Respect of Joint Company Secretaries
kg_typical_structure.description: Details the appointment of joint company secretaries who may not meet the strict qualifications under Rule 3.28 of the Listing Rules.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Waiver in Relation to Management Presence in Hong Kong
kg_typical_structure.description: Provides justification for the lack of sufficient management presence in Hong Kong and outlines the measures taken to maintain regular and effective communication with the Stock Exchange.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Waiver in Relation to Continuing Connected Transactions
kg_typical_structure.description: Details any ongoing or expected connected transactions and the waivers granted for compliance with Chapter 14A of the Listing Rules.
Includes any other specific waivers or exemptions granted for compliance with various Listing Rules or other relevant ordinances.

kg_writing_rules: Use formal and precise language consistent with the Listing Rules and HKEX guidelines.
Clearly state the specific rule or ordinance being waived or exempted.
Provide detailed explanations for the necessity of the waiver or exemption.
Include the date of application and the date of grant for each waiver or exemption.
Cross-reference relevant sections of the prospectus for additional information.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The date the waiver or exemption was granted by HKEX.
The specific rule or ordinance being waived or exempted.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Rule 8.17 of the Listing Rules

kg_required_input_fields.description: The reason for seeking the waiver or exemption.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Management presence in Hong Kong is impractical due to the company's operations in the PRC.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The expiration date of the waiver or exemption.
The entity granting the waiver or exemption.
kg_required_input_fields.example: The Stock Exchange

kg_evidence_types: Appointment letter of joint company secretaries
kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to provide a clear and detailed explanation for the necessity of the waiver or exemption.
Not including the date of application and grant for each waiver or exemption.
Omitting cross-references to relevant sections of the prospectus.
Not updating the waiver or exemption details if the situation changes post-listing.

InfoProspectus.name: Information about this Prospectus and the Global Offering
InfoProspectus.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide key reliance, distribution, and offering-notice language for the prospectus and the Global Offering.

Required structure:
- Reliance-only-on-this-prospectus language.
- No-authorised-other-information language.
- Territorial restrictions and no-public-offer-outside-permitted-jurisdictions wording if supported.
- Currency conversion note where relevant.
- Listing, trading, and CCASS or settlement context if supported.
- Website-not-part-of-the-prospectus wording where website references appear. Any section that mentions a website must state that the website does not form part of the prospectus where appropriate.
- Directors' responsibility wording if supported.

Controls:
- Use formal explanatory prose, not marketing language. No hidden profit-forecast implications.
- Do not imply a profit forecast, valuation certainty, or offering completion certainty.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] and [[AI:XREF|to=GlobalOfferingStructure]] where helpful.
- Missing notices should remain visible as [Information not provided in the documents] plus [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm standard reliance, territorial restriction, and website-not-part wording.]].
InfoProspectus.kg_function: To detail the responsibilities of the directors, the structure and conditions of the global offering, and to ensure the accuracy and completeness of the prospectus.
InfoProspectus.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and accuracy in the prospectus, providing investors with critical information about the offering, the roles of various parties involved, and the conditions under which the shares will be offered. This section also clarifies the legal and regulatory compliance of the offering and addresses potential tax implications and exchange rate conversions.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Directors’ Responsibility for the Contents of this Prospectus
kg_typical_structure.description: States the responsibility of the directors for the contents of the prospectus and confirms the accuracy and completeness of the information provided.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Information on the Global Offering, Structure and Conditions of the Global Offering and Procedures for Application for Hong Kong Offer Shares
kg_typical_structure.description: Describes the global offering, including the number of shares, the structure, and the application process for Hong Kong offer shares.
Provides translations of certain amounts denominated in different currencies for convenience and clarifies that no representation is made regarding actual conversion rates.
Clarifies the precedence of the English version in case of inconsistencies with the Chinese translation.
Explains that certain amounts and percentage figures have been subject to rounding adjustments.
Details the arrangement relating to the over-allotment option and stabilization if applicable.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Ensure all information is accurate and complete.
Cross-reference with other sections of the prospectus where appropriate.
Adhere to the Companies (Winding Up and Miscellaneous Provisions) Ordinance, the Securities and Futures (Stock Market Listing) Rules, and the Listing Rules.
Include translations for amounts denominated in different currencies.
Specify the precedence of the English version in case of inconsistencies with the Chinese translation.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Statement confirming the directors' responsibility for the contents of the prospectus.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Our Directors, having made all reasonable enquiries, confirm that, to the best of their knowledge and belief, the information contained in this prospectus is accurate and complete in all material respects and not misleading or deceptive.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Number of Hong Kong offer shares to be issued.
Number of international offer shares to be issued.
Details of the underwriting agreement, including the names of the underwriters and the terms of the agreement.
kg_required_input_fields.example: The Hong Kong Offer Shares are offered solely on the basis of the information contained and representations made in this prospectus and the Application Forms and on the terms and subject to the conditions set out herein and therein.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Exchange rate conversions for amounts denominated in different currencies.
kg_required_input_fields.example: The translations between Renminbi and Hong Kong dollars were made at the rate of RMB0.78623 to HK$1.00.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Precedence of the English version in case of inconsistencies with the Chinese translation.
kg_required_input_fields.example: If there is any inconsistency between this prospectus and the Chinese translation of this prospectus, this prospectus shall prevail.

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to accurately represent the directors' responsibility for the contents of the prospectus.
Omitting important information about the global offering structure and conditions.
Incorrect exchange rate conversions.
Inconsistencies between the English and Chinese versions of the prospectus.
Failure to disclose all relevant tax implications.

DirectorsParties.name: Directors and Parties Involved in the Global Offering

Role: Provide a directory of directors and the professional parties involved in the transaction. Exact names and roles; directory style, not narrative style.

Required structure:

- Professional parties such as sponsors, coordinators, bookrunners, lead managers, legal advisers, reporting accountants or auditors, valuers, compliance advisers, receiving banks, and registrars if supported.
- Use a list or table rather than narrative prose. Consistency with Underwriting and appendices.

Controls:
- Names and roles must match the source documents exactly. Do not invent roles, firm names, or addresses.
- Evidence references: where available, tie to engagement letters or official appointment records.
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Underwriting]] where party names must reconcile across sections.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm official names and appointments against engagement letters or formal appointment records.]] when any entry is incomplete.
DirectorsParties.kg_function: To disclose the key individuals and entities involved in the global offering process, including directors, parties involved, and their roles.
DirectorsParties.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and provide investors with comprehensive information about the management and advisory teams involved in the global offering. CorporateInfo.name: Corporate Information
CorporateInfo.requirements:

- Website reference with a statement that the website does not form part of the prospectus.
- Company secretary, authorised representatives, compliance adviser, registrar, stock code, and similar particulars as applicable and supported.

Controls:
- Use structured list or table format. Do not infer missing corporate particulars.
- Add [[AI:LPD|refresh=corporate particulars and officers]] for information likely to change before filing.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Confirm incorporation, registered office, company secretary, and registrar details from corporate records.]] when required.
CorporateInfo.kg_function: To provide essential corporate details and governance information for potential investors.
CorporateInfo.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and provide investors with comprehensive information about the issuer's corporate structure, key personnel, and governance mechanisms. Regulation.name: Regulation (Regulatory Overview)
Regulation.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Summarise the principal laws, licences, approvals, restrictions, and compliance status by topic that materially affect the business.

Required structure:
- Organise by regulatory topic: licensing and approvals, data privacy and cybersecurity (including data lifecycle and personal-data logic if relevant), internet information services, foreign ownership restrictions, IP, consumer or product regulation, and other sector-specific rules as supported.
- For each topic state the main rule set, the relevant regulator, the core requirement, and the issuer's compliance status if supported. Material incidents if relevant and evidenced.

Controls:
- Cross-reference Business and Risk Factors using [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where relevant.
- For technology issuers, do not omit data privacy and cybersecurity logic, personal-data lifecycle controls, or material incidents if supported by the documents.
- Do not downplay regulatory risk or state legal conclusions without support.
- Add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain legal opinions, licence evidence, filing records, and incident logs.]] where required.
Regulation.kg_function: To summarize the key regulatory frameworks affecting the issuer's business in the PRC.
Regulation.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure that potential investors have a clear understanding of the regulatory environment in which the issuer operates, particularly focusing on PRC laws and regulations that significantly impact the issuer's business activities, financial performance, and compliance obligations.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Regulations on Value-Added Telecommunications Services and Foreign Investment Restrictions
kg_typical_structure.description: Details on foreign investment regulations, including the latest versions of the Negative List and Catalogs of Encouraged Industries.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Government Policies Related to the Issuer's Industry
kg_typical_structure.description: Summaries of government policies and initiatives that support the industry in which the issuer operates.
Information on regulations governing product liability, consumer protection, and safety standards.

kg_typical_structure.subsection: Cross-Border Transactions and Foreign Exchange Regulations
kg_typical_structure.description: Details on regulations related to cross-border transactions, foreign exchange controls, and overseas listings.
Summaries of laws and regulations related to intellectual property rights and data security.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Ensure all references to laws and regulations are up-to-date.
Provide specific examples where applicable.
Cross-reference with other sections of the prospectus as necessary.
Maintain a neutral tone and avoid promotional language.

kg_required_input_fields.description: The most recent version of the Negative List for Foreign Investment Access.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Special Administrative Measures (Negative List) for Foreign Investment Access (2020 Version)

kg_required_input_fields.description: The current version of the Catalog of Industries for Encouraging Foreign Investment.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Catalog of Industries for Encouraging Foreign Investment (2020 Version)

kg_required_input_fields.description: Key government policies supporting the issuer's industry.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Made in China 2025 Plan

kg_required_input_fields.description: Laws and regulations related to product liability and consumer protection.
kg_required_input_fields.example: PRC Product Quality Law

kg_required_input_fields.description: Regulations governing cross-border transactions and foreign exchange controls.
kg_required_input_fields.example: SAFE Circular 28

kg_common_pitfalls: Failing to update references to outdated laws and regulations.
Omitting critical regulatory requirements that could impact the issuer's business.
Providing vague or overly general descriptions of regulations.
Neglecting to include relevant government policies that support the issuer's industry.

IndustryOverview.name: Industry Overview
IndustryOverview.requirements:
Draft in sponsor-counsel working draft mode, not as a fully complete final filing copy.

Role: Provide an objective market background only. No company promotion disguised as industry analysis. Clear third-party attribution for all market data.

Required structure:
- Define the industry or relevant market segments.
- Present market size, growth, demand drivers, and competitive landscape only if supported by sourced materials.
- For each sourced statistic define scope, metric, date, and period explicitly.
- Include concentration, ranking, share, barriers to entry, or outlook only if supported by sourced materials. Use tables or concise prose as the data allows.

Controls:
- Every market statistic, projection, ranking, share, or CAGR must carry [[AI:CITE|source=...; scope=...; date=...; metric=...]] with defined scope, metric, date, and period.
- Do not present unsourced leadership claims, estimated growth, or third-party projections as issuer facts.
- If sourcing metadata is incomplete, keep the statement as [Information not provided in the documents] and add [[AI:VERIFY|Obtain full third-party source, scope, metric, and date.]].
- Add [[AI:XREF|to=Business]] and [[AI:XREF|to=Risk Factors]] where competitive or market statements connect to company disclosure.
IndustryOverview.kg_function: To provide an overview of the industry in which the issuer operates, including relevant market data, trends, and competitive landscape.
IndustryOverview.kg_purpose: HKEX requires this section to ensure transparency and provide potential investors with a clear understanding of the industry dynamics, market size, growth prospects, and competitive environment. Regulators and investors expect to see reliable and up-to-date information that supports the issuer's business strategy and financial projections.

kg_typical_structure.description: Brief introduction to the industry, highlighting its significance and relevance to the issuer.
Detailed analysis of the current market size, growth rates, and projected future trends.
Discussion of the main factors driving industry growth and the challenges faced by the industry.
Overview of major competitors, market share, and competitive strategies.
Explanation of relevant regulations and their impact on the industry.
Discussion of emerging technologies and their potential impact on the industry.

kg_writing_rules: Use clear and concise language.
Avoid overly technical jargon unless necessary.
Provide cross-references to other sections of the prospectus where relevant information is discussed.
Ensure all data and statistics are accurate and up-to-date.
Disclose any conflicts of interest related to the sources of information.

kg_required_input_fields.description: Name of the industry in which the issuer operates.
kg_required_input_fields.example: Artificial Intelligence

kg_required_input_fields.description: Current market size of the industry.
